## 🍵 Introduction

> This plugin depends on [typedoc-plugin-navigation-hooks](https://www.npmjs.com/package/typedoc-plugin-navigation-hooks)
> **experimental** plugin.

It adds support for **@navigationTitle** [JSDoc](https://jsdoc.app/) tag to
manually set the title displayed in the navigation for a reflection.

Just add tag to the comment:

```js
/**
 * @navigationTitle Awesome foo!
 */
export function foo () {}
```

If `foo` has its own page and navigation item, the title displayed in
navigation will be `"Awesome foo!"`.

> Note that the reflections will have the `_navigationTitle` property set.
> See [`Reflection`](#reflection) type below.

## 🚀 Get started

```sh
npm install -D typedoc-plugin-navigation-title
```

Add the plugins in your `typedoc.config.js` file:

```js
// typedoc.config.js

export default {
  entryPoints: [
    './src/index.ts'
  ],
  plugin: [
    'typedoc-plugin-navigation-hooks',
    'typedoc-plugin-navigation-title'
  ]
}
```

> 💡 You don't need to add "@navigationTitle" to TypeDoc `blockTags` option

Let's run the `typedoc` command!

## 🧬 Types

### Reflection

```ts
export interface Reflection extends TypeDoc.Reflection {
  /**
   * The title to display in navigation
   */
  _navigationTitle?: string
}

```

## 🧰 TSDoc configuration

If you use a `tsdoc.json` configuration file in your project, you can
extend your own config with this plugin TSDoc config file:

```json
// tsdoc.json
{
  "$schema": "https://developer.microsoft.com/json-schemas/tsdoc/v0/tsdoc.schema.json",
  "extends": [
    "@microsoft/api-extractor/extends/tsdoc-base.json",
    "typedoc/tsdoc.json",
    "typedoc-plugin-navigation-title/extends/tsdoc.json"
  ]
}
```

> 👀 See also the [TypeDoc tags](https://typedoc.org/documents/Tags.html#defining-tags)
> documentation.
